toolUseResult.summary: Parallel rewrite wave: rewrite each test file to 10/10 RUBRIC, verify green (no mutation). Same tree, disjoint test files.

content.subject: Build + self-validate mutation-testing harness (objective quality gate)
content.description: Build a deterministic AST-based mutation harness: given a production file + its targeting test(s), generate semantic mutants (comparison flips, boolean swaps, constant/return changes, off-by-one, branch removal), apply each, run targeted tests, record kill/survive, revert. Output JSON kill-rate report. This is the OBJECTIVE quality gate — not an LLM opinion. Then META-VALIDATE it: run on a known-good test (must show high kill rate) and a known-weak test (must show survivors). If it can't distinguish, the harness is broken — fix before use.

metadata.finding: Harness built (stdlib AST mutator) + self-validated. weather_tool=50% kill (weak confirmed), search_service=24% kill (LLM audit called it SOLID/best — objectively WRONG), registry=baseline RED (2 tests already failing on develop). Conclusion: LLM scoring is unreliable; mutation kill-rate is the gate.

content.subject: Write 10/10 test rubric + behavior-spec template
content.description: Codify the non-negotiable quality bar: deletion test, mutation kill-rate threshold, mock-at-boundary, real-behavior assertions, branch+error coverage, no escape-hatch assertions, fewer-but-stronger. Plus a behavior-spec template capturing 'what the code is expected to do / what actually happens / what must be caught if broken' per production unit. This is the contract every rewritten file must satisfy.

metadata.artifact: .agents/plans/test-overhaul/RUBRIC.md — hard gates: green-isolated+parallel baseline, mutation kill >=90% (100% critical-path), import-prod, mock-at-boundary, real-behavior asserts, all branches+errors, no escape-hatch asserts, no redundant tests. Behavior-spec format = EXPECTED/MECHANISM/MUST-CATCH.

content.subject: Baseline existing suite: inventory + per-module mutation scoreboard
content.description: Map every test file to the production module(s) it targets across all apps/libs. Run the mutation harness against the EXISTING tests per module to produce an objective scoreboard of current kill rate (proof of how little the current suite catches). Triage each into KEEP / REWRITE / DELETE / GAP.

content.subject: Overhaul tests module-by-module via agent pipeline (resumable)
content.description: For each production module: write behavior spec -> rewrite tests to 10/10 -> run green against real code/services -> mutation-verify (must kill all meaningful mutants) -> consolidate to fewer/stronger tests. Pipeline of agents (spec-writer, rewriter, runner, mutation-verifier, scorer). Loop a file until kill rate hits threshold. Track in a manifest for resumability. Covers every app and lib.

metadata.pilot: search_service: 24%->90.4% kill, 14->9 tests, green isolated+parallel, autonomous via subagent+harness. 9 survivors empirically proven equivalent. Pattern: real-Mongo via mongodb_url fixture + monkeypatch the module's own collection bindings. Proven the agent+harness loop works.

content.subject: Cover untested backend production code with new 10/10 tests
content.description: Identify production code (especially apps/api backend) with no or low real coverage. For each meaningful unit, add mutation-verified 10/10 tests. 'Each part of the code in the backend' must be evaluated, not just files that already have tests.

content.subject: Wire CI quality gate: coverage + mutation-kill threshold
content.description: Wire the PR-blocking CI path to enforce coverage (--cov-fail-under, currently unwired) and a mutation-kill threshold so future regressions are caught and untested code cannot merge. Make the gate real, not advisory.

content.subject: Final independent meta-audit of overhauled suite
content.description: Independent agent re-verifies a random sample of 'passed' files by injecting fresh, unseen bugs into production code and confirming the new tests catch them. Guards against the harness/pipeline grading itself too generously. If any injected bug survives, the bar was too low — loop back.

content.subject: Map architecture + rank every module by blast-radius/criticality
content.description: Use code-review-graph (get_architecture_overview, get_impact_radius, get_hub_nodes, get_affected_flows) + parallel subagents to map the entire backend (and apps/libs) and rank production modules by 'how badly do users get hurt if this breaks'. Output a prioritized critical-path list: tool-data/SSE streaming, agent graph routing, auth/token, payments, conversation persistence, MCP, rate limiting, webhooks, etc. This ordering drives test-overhaul priority. Account for recent develop changes (ToolCallsSection.tsx deleted, executor_tool test removed).

metadata.artifact: .agents/plans/test-overhaul/PRIORITY.md — 15 P0 critical-path modules ranked. Tool-data/SSE contract (stream_utils.py) = P0 #2. Untested P0/P1 gaps: agent_auth.verify_agent_token (JWT security), retry_policies, webhook_utils HMAC.

content.subject: Critical-path contract tests: tool-data / SSE streaming contract
content.description: Explicit user priority. Map the full tool-data path end to end: tool writer payloads (e.g. writer({"weather_data":..., "location":...}) keys), process_data_chunk, StreamManager publish/subscribe, SSE frame shape, agent event stream, and the frontend consumer contract. Write mutation-verified contract tests so renaming/breaking a streamed key or SSE frame is caught. The weather_tool mutation run proved these frontend keys are currently UNtested (survived mutation).

content.subject: Fix already-broken + flaky/non-isolated tests; enforce isolation
content.description: The suite is NOT green on develop: test_registry.py has 2 failing tests in normal runs, and a 3rd that only fails in isolation (order/shared-state leak via lazy_loader KeyError). Find all already-failing tests and all order/parallelism-dependent tests. A 10/10 test MUST pass both in isolation (-n0) and in parallel (-n 4), deterministically. Make 'passes in isolation' a hard criterion in the rubric and harness baseline.

content.subject: Build the multi-agent overhaul Workflow (spec->rewrite->run->mutate->score)
content.description: Build the resumable Workflow that IS the 'team of agents' the user asked for: spec-writer (behavior contract) -> rewriter (10/10 tests) -> runner (green in isolation+parallel) -> mutation-verifier (kill rate >= threshold via mutate.py) -> scorer. Loop each file until kill rate hits the bar. Pipeline across files with concurrency. Persist a manifest for resume. Reject any rewrite the harness can't certify.

content.subject: Overhaul TypeScript suites (bots, web, cli, wake-word) with objective gate
content.description: Every app/lib included. Bring the TS suites (apps/bots, apps/web, packages/cli, libs/wake-word) to the same 10/10 bar with an objective mutation gate (Stryker mutator for TS, or an equivalent AST mutator). Kill the tautological 'spy-on-own-method' tests the audit found (discord/slack adapters) and the hardcoded-count snapshot tests (genericLibrary).

content.subject: Overhaul libs/shared (py + ts) tests + fix runnability
content.description: libs/shared/py tests currently fail to run in isolation (shared.py package shadows stdlib logging -> circular import unless run from repo root). Fix runnability so they execute deterministically, then overhaul to 10/10 with mutation verification. Cover libs/shared/ts as well.

content.subject: Consolidation pass: delete hollow/redundant tests (quality over quantity)
content.description: User mandate: fewer, INSANELY good tests, not 8,988 mediocre ones. input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Serial mutation GATE over the rewritten suite. Phase 2 of the overhaul.

Runs mutate.py one target at a time (safe: no concurrent prod-file edits), records
kill_rate + baseline_green per target into the manifest, and flags below-gate files
for the patch wave. Resumable: re-run to fill only missing/failed rows.

Run from apps/api:
    MONGODB_URL='mongodb://localhost:27017/gaia_test' \
    uv run python ../../.agents/plans/test-overhaul/gate_runner.py \
        ../../.agents/plans/test-overhaul/targets.json \
        ../../.agents/plans/test-overhaul/manifest.json [--only-below] [--redo]
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

MUTATE = str(Path(__file__).parent / "mutate.py")
DB_TIERS = {"service", "services", "integration", "db", "mcp", "vfs", "agents", "api"}


def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("targets")
    ap.add_argument("manifest")
    ap.add_argument("--root", default=".")
    ap.add_argument("--only-below", action="store_true", help="only re-gate rows currently below gate")
    ap.add_argument("--redo", action="store_true", help="ignore existing rows, re-gate all")
    ap.add_argument("--max-p0", type=int, default=24)
    ap.add_argument("--max-default", type=int, default=14)
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=60)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    targets = json.loads(Path(args.targets).read_text())
    man_path = Path(args.manifest)
    manifest: dict = json.loads(man_path.read_text()) if man_path.exists() else {}

    def needs_run(t: dict) -> bool:
        key = t["test_file"]
        row = manifest.get(key)
        if args.redo:
            return True
        if row is None:
            return True
        if args.only_below:
            kr = row.get("kill_rate")
            return kr is not None and kr < t["gate"]
        return row.get("kill_rate") is None  # only fill missing
    todo = [t for t in targets if needs_run(t)]
    print(f"targets: {len(targets)} | to gate: {len(todo)}", file=sys.stderr)

    for i, t in enumerate(todo, 1):
        prod = Path(args.root) / t["prod_file"]
        if not prod.exists():
            manifest[t["test_file"]] = {**manifest.get(t["test_file"], {}), "error": "prod-missing", "prod_file": t["prod_file"]}
            man_path.write_text(json.dumps(manifest, indent=2))
            continue
        cap = args.max_p0 if t["gate"] >= 1.0 else args.max_default
        cmd = [
            sys.executable, MUTATE, t["prod_file"],
            "--tests", t["test_file"],
            "--max", str(cap), "--timeout", str(args.timeout),
            "--root", args.root, "--quiet",
        ]
        try:
            proc = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=args.timeout * (cap + 5))
            report = json.loads(proc.stdout) if proc.stdout.strip().startswith("{") else {"error": "no-json", "stderr": proc.stderr[-300:]}
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            report = {"error": "harness-timeout"}
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
            report = {"error": str(exc)}

        kr = report.get("kill_rate")
        bg = report.get("baseline_green")
        manifest[t["test_file"]] = {
            "prod_file": t["prod_file"],
            "tier": t["tier"],
            "gate": t["gate"],
            "baseline_green": bg,
            "kill_rate": kr,
            "survived": report.get("survived"),
            "executed": report.get("executed"),
            "below_gate": (kr is not None and kr < t["gate"]),
            "survivors": report.get("survivors", []),
            "error": report.get("error"),
        }
        man_path.write_text(json.dumps(manifest, indent=2))
        flag = "RED-BASE" if bg is False else (f"kill={kr} {'BELOW' if kr is not None and kr < t['gate'] else 'OK'}" if kr is not None else f"ERR:{report.get('error')}")
        print(f"[{i}/{len(todo)}] {Path(t['test_file']).name} ({t['tier']}, gate {t['gate']}) -> {flag}", file=sys.stderr)

    rows = list(manifest.values())
    graded = [r for r in rows if r.get("kill_rate") is not None]
    below = [r for r in graded if r.get("below_gate")]
    red = [r for r in rows if r.get("baseline_green") is False]
    print(f"DONE. graded={len(graded)} below_gate={len(below)} red_baseline={len(red)}", file=sys.stderr)
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

            report = json.loads(proc.stdout) if proc.stdout.strip().startswith("{") else {"error": "no-json", "stderr": proc.stderr[-300:]}
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            report = {"error": "harness-timeout"}

        kr = report.get("kill_rate")

content.text:
Everything that can be built without touching the apps/api tree is now ready: the **rewrite workflow** (args bug fixed), the **serial mutation gate**, and the **patch workflow**. The 3-target smoke test of the hardest P0 files is still running — I won't scale to 246 until I've seen its output quality and run the gate on its results, per "every step perfect."

Here's the consolidated state while that completes:

**Built & proven**
- Mutation harness (objective gate) — self-validated; exposed that LLM scoring is unreliable (the "SOLID" file killed 24%).
- Pilot: `search_service` 24%→90.4%, 14→9 tests, green isolated+parallel, committed.
- Architecture map → 246 targets queued, 24 P0 first; tool-data/SSE + 3 untested gaps identified.

**Execution model (locked, per your guidance — no worktrees)**
- Phase 1: parallel rewrite (10 agents, same tree, disjoint test files, `.venv/bin/python` directly to avoid venv races) → green isolated+parallel.
- Phase 2: serial mutation gate → kill-rate per file, flags below-gate.
- Phase 3: parallel patch wave → close survivors to ≥gate (100% P0).
- Then: TS suites, libs/shared, untested-code coverage, CI gate, independent meta-audit, tests/CLAUDE.md, skill PR, then commit+push and open the PR to `develop`.

**On the branch** `chore/test-suite-overhaul`; pilot committed.

The smoke test will auto-notify me on completion — at which point I evaluate the 3 rewrites, gate them, and if quality holds, launch the full 246-target wave to run through the night. I'll keep going automatically.

Added a full behavior-spec docstring (EXPECTED / MECHANISM / MUST-CATCH).

Boundary mocking only: construct_langchain_messages, GraphManager.get_graph, store_user_message_memory, execute_graph_streaming, execute_graph_silent, trace_id_for_message, and log are patched on the real module via patch.object. build_initial_state / build_agent_config are sibling collaborators (in app/helpers/agent_helpers.py) patched so the tests assert exactly what agent.py forwards into them and how it uses the return value — the behaviour under test.

Branches/contracts now covered that the old suite missed:
- get_graph requested for the literal "comms_agent" (kills name-mutation).
- active_todo_id precedence: "active_todo_id" wins over "todo_id"; falls back to "todo_id"; None with no trigger.
- execution_mode whitelist: "background" accepted, an out-of-set value ("turbo") falls back to "interactive", interactive default with no trigger.
- resolved active_todo_id/execution_mode propagate to BOTH build_initial_state (via trigger_context positional arg) AND build_agent_config (kwargs) AND construct_langchain_messages.
- build_initial_state receives user_id coerced to "" when user_id is None, plus conversation_id and the constructed history identity.

content.text:
Smoke test **completed: 3/3 rewritten, 0 failed, all green isolated + parallel.** And the agents exceeded the brief:
- `test_agent_core`: 20→30 tests, full behavior spec, every branch/contract.
- `test_agent_routing`: 9→12, **deleted a hollow structure-snapshot class**, drives the *real* compiled graph, and **flagged a genuine prod smell** (duplicated sync/async logic where the sync copies are dead code).
- `test_graph_builder`: 48→54, covered a **previously-untested factory**.

But per the core principle — **I don't trust the agents' self-reports.** Let me independently confirm green and run the objective mutation gate on all 3 (these are P0, gate=1.0). `agent_core` and `graph_builder` didn't self-mutate, so the gate is the real test.

input.command:
cd /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia/apps/api
M=/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia/.agents/plans/test-overhaul/mutate.py
for pair in \
  "app/agents/core/agent.py|tests/unit/agents/test_agent_core.py" \
  "app/override/langgraph_bigtool/create_agent.py|tests/unit/agents/test_agent_routing.py" \
  "app/agents/core/graph_builder/checkpointer_manager.py|tests/unit/agents/test_graph_builder.py"; do
  prod="${pair%%|*}"; test="${pair##*|}"
  MONGODB_URL='mongodb://localhost:27017/gaia_test' uv run python "$M" "$prod" --tests "$test" --max 25 --timeout 60 --root . --quiet --json /tmp/gate_$(basename $test).json >/dev/null 2>&1
  python3 -c "
import json;d=json.load(open('/tmp/gate_$(basename $test).json'))
print(f\"{'$test'.split('/')[-1]:32} baseline_green={d['baseline_green']} kill={d['kill_rate']} (killed {d['killed']}/{d['executed']}, survived {d['survived']})\")"
done

content.subject: Build + self-validate mutation-testing harness (objective quality gate)
content.description: Build a deterministic AST-based mutation harness: given a production file + its targeting test(s), generate semantic mutants (comparison flips, boolean swaps, constant/return changes, off-by-one, branch removal), apply each, run targeted tests, record kill/survive, revert. Output JSON kill-rate report. This is the OBJECTIVE quality gate — not an LLM opinion. Then META-VALIDATE it: run on a known-good test (must show high kill rate) and a known-weak test (must show survivors). If it can't distinguish, the harness is broken — fix before use.

metadata.finding: Harness built (stdlib AST mutator) + self-validated. weather_tool=50% kill (weak confirmed), search_service=24% kill (LLM audit called it SOLID/best — objectively WRONG), registry=baseline RED (2 tests already failing on develop). Conclusion: LLM scoring is unreliable; mutation kill-rate is the gate.

content.subject: Write 10/10 test rubric + behavior-spec template
content.description: Codify the non-negotiable quality bar: deletion test, mutation kill-rate threshold, mock-at-boundary, real-behavior assertions, branch+error coverage, no escape-hatch assertions, fewer-but-stronger. Plus a behavior-spec template capturing 'what the code is expected to do / what actually happens / what must be caught if broken' per production unit. This is the contract every rewritten file must satisfy.

metadata.artifact: .agents/plans/test-overhaul/RUBRIC.md — hard gates: green-isolated+parallel baseline, mutation kill >=90% (100% critical-path), import-prod, mock-at-boundary, real-behavior asserts, all branches+errors, no escape-hatch asserts, no redundant tests. Behavior-spec format = EXPECTED/MECHANISM/MUST-CATCH.

content.subject: Baseline existing suite: inventory + per-module mutation scoreboard
content.description: Map every test file to the production module(s) it targets across all apps/libs. Run the mutation harness against the EXISTING tests per module to produce an objective scoreboard of current kill rate (proof of how little the current suite catches). Triage each into KEEP / REWRITE / DELETE / GAP.

content.subject: Overhaul tests module-by-module via agent pipeline (resumable)
content.description: For each production module: write behavior spec -> rewrite tests to 10/10 -> run green against real code/services -> mutation-verify (must kill all meaningful mutants) -> consolidate to fewer/stronger tests. Pipeline of agents (spec-writer, rewriter, runner, mutation-verifier, scorer). Loop a file until kill rate hits threshold. Track in a manifest for resumability. Covers every app and lib.

metadata.pilot: search_service: 24%->90.4% kill, 14->9 tests, green isolated+parallel, autonomous via subagent+harness. 9 survivors empirically proven equivalent. Pattern: real-Mongo via mongodb_url fixture + monkeypatch the module's own collection bindings. Proven the agent+harness loop works.

content.subject: Cover untested backend production code with new 10/10 tests
content.description: Identify production code (especially apps/api backend) with no or low real coverage. For each meaningful unit, add mutation-verified 10/10 tests. 'Each part of the code in the backend' must be evaluated, not just files that already have tests.

content.subject: Wire CI quality gate: coverage + mutation-kill threshold
content.description: Wire the PR-blocking CI path to enforce coverage (--cov-fail-under, currently unwired) and a mutation-kill threshold so future regressions are caught and untested code cannot merge. Make the gate real, not advisory.

content.subject: Final independent meta-audit of overhauled suite
content.description: Independent agent re-verifies a random sample of 'passed' files by injecting fresh, unseen bugs into production code and confirming the new tests catch them. Guards against the harness/pipeline grading itself too generously. If any injected bug survives, the bar was too low — loop back.

content.subject: Map architecture + rank every module by blast-radius/criticality
content.description: Use code-review-graph (get_architecture_overview, get_impact_radius, get_hub_nodes, get_affected_flows) + parallel subagents to map the entire backend (and apps/libs) and rank production modules by 'how badly do users get hurt if this breaks'. Output a prioritized critical-path list: tool-data/SSE streaming, agent graph routing, auth/token, payments, conversation persistence, MCP, rate limiting, webhooks, etc. This ordering drives test-overhaul priority. Account for recent develop changes (ToolCallsSection.tsx deleted, executor_tool test removed).

metadata.artifact: .agents/plans/test-overhaul/PRIORITY.md — 15 P0 critical-path modules ranked. Tool-data/SSE contract (stream_utils.py) = P0 #2. Untested P0/P1 gaps: agent_auth.verify_agent_token (JWT security), retry_policies, webhook_utils HMAC.

content.subject: Critical-path contract tests: tool-data / SSE streaming contract
content.description: Explicit user priority. Map the full tool-data path end to end: tool writer payloads (e.g. writer({"weather_data":..., "location":...}) keys), process_data_chunk, StreamManager publish/subscribe, SSE frame shape, agent event stream, and the frontend consumer contract. Write mutation-verified contract tests so renaming/breaking a streamed key or SSE frame is caught. The weather_tool mutation run proved these frontend keys are currently UNtested (survived mutation).

content.subject: Fix already-broken + flaky/non-isolated tests; enforce isolation
content.description: The suite is NOT green on develop: test_registry.py has 2 failing tests in normal runs, and a 3rd that only fails in isolation (order/shared-state leak via lazy_loader KeyError). Find all already-failing tests and all order/parallelism-dependent tests. A 10/10 test MUST pass both in isolation (-n0) and in parallel (-n 4), deterministically. Make 'passes in isolation' a hard criterion in the rubric and harness baseline.

content.subject: Build the multi-agent overhaul Workflow (spec->rewrite->run->mutate->score)
content.description: Build the resumable Workflow that IS the 'team of agents' the user asked for: spec-writer (behavior contract) -> rewriter (10/10 tests) -> runner (green in isolation+parallel) -> mutation-verifier (kill rate >= threshold via mutate.py) -> scorer. Loop each file until kill rate hits the bar. Pipeline across files with concurrency. Persist a manifest for resume. Reject any rewrite the harness can't certify.

content.subject: Overhaul TypeScript suites (bots, web, cli, wake-word) with objective gate
content.description: Every app/lib included. Bring the TS suites (apps/bots, apps/web, packages/cli, libs/wake-word) to the same 10/10 bar with an objective mutation gate (Stryker mutator for TS, or an equivalent AST mutator). Kill the tautological 'spy-on-own-method' tests the audit found (discord/slack adapters) and the hardcoded-count snapshot tests (genericLibrary).

content.subject: Overhaul libs/shared (py + ts) tests + fix runnability
content.description: libs/shared/py tests currently fail to run in isolation (shared.py package shadows stdlib logging -> circular import unless run from repo root). Fix runnability so they execute deterministically, then overhaul to 10/10 with mutation verification. Cover libs/shared/ts as well.

content.subject: Consolidation pass: delete hollow/redundant tests (quality over quantity)
content.description: User mandate: fewer, INSANELY good tests, not 8,988 mediocre ones. After mutation scoring, delete hollow tests (those that survive deletion of prod code / contribute no unique mutant kills) and merge redundant ones. Net test count should drop while kill rate rises. Track before/after counts.

content.subject: Maintain live scoreboard/manifest of every file AND every test function
content.description: User requires every single test file AND every test function evaluated without fail. Maintain a manifest (JSON/CSV) listing each test file + each production unit: evaluated yes/no, baseline status, kill-rate before/after, verdict (KEEP/REWRITE/DELETE/GAP), owner agent. This is the proof of total coverage of the evaluation itself and drives resumability.

content.subject: Document the session with log-convo skill (final step)
content.description: At the very end, invoke the log-convo skill to document this test-suite overhaul session as an agent-convos entry. Explicit user request.

content.subject: Open PR to develop after overhaul complete
content.description: At the very end (after all tests overhauled, green, mutation-verified, CI gate wired, meta-audit passed, and changes committed+pushed on chore/test-suite-overhaul), open a PR targeting develop. Do NOT merge it (humans merge). Include before/after kill-rate scoreboard + test-count reduction in the PR body.

content.subject: Write apps/api/tests/CLAUDE.md (test quality guide)
content.description: At the end, write a CLAUDE.md in the tests folder codifying what makes a good test case, how to evaluate one (mutation kill-rate / deletion test), how to write one (behavior spec, mock-at-boundary, real-behavior asserts, branch+error coverage), and how to develop with them (run isolated+parallel, the harness, the gates). Derived from the RUBRIC + everything learned this session. Auto-loads when working in tests/.

content.subject: Write + PR a test-quality skill to theexperiencecompany/skills
content.description: At the end, author a skill in the theexperiencecompany/skills repo capturing the methodology: understanding what makes good test cases, evaluating them (mutation testing as ground truth, not LLM opinion), writing them (behavior specs, the 5 laws, anti-patterns), and developing with them. Open a PR to that repo. Reuse the mutation harness + RUBRIC as the skill's backbone.

lastPrompt: 25-40 hours is unacceptable. please use subagents and agents. ensure that every single step is done perfectly and efficiently by the morning once i'm awake. ensure extremely utmost high quality btw.

content.text: `uv run` triggered a sync that hit a symlink-loop error — likely my earlier worktree probe's `gaia-shared` rebuild repointed the editable install. But the smoke agents ran fine because they used `.venv/bin/python` **directly** (no sync). The fix is simple: never use `uv run` — use the venv Python directly everywhere (as mutate.py and the agents already do). Let me confirm the venv imports are healthy, then re-run the gate correctly.

input.command:
cd /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia/apps/api
VENVPY=/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia/.venv/bin/python
echo "=== venv import health (direct python, no uv) ==="
$VENVPY -c "import app.agents.core.agent; import shared.py.wide_events; print('imports OK')" 2>&1 | tail -1
echo ""
echo "=== gate on 3 smoke targets (direct venv python) ==="
M=/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia/.agents/plans/test-overhaul/mutate.py
for pair in \
  "app/agents/core/agent.py|tests/unit/agents/test_agent_core.py" \
  "app/override/langgraph_bigtool/create_agent.py|tests/unit/agents/test_agent_routing.py" \
  "app/agents/core/graph_builder/checkpointer_manager.py|tests/unit/agents/test_graph_builder.py"; do
  prod="${pair%%|*}"; test="${pair##*|}"; b=$(basename $test)
  MONGODB_URL='mongodb://localhost:27017/gaia_test' $VENVPY "$M" "$prod" --tests "$test" --max 25 --timeout 60 --root . --quiet --json /tmp/gate_$b.json >/dev/null 2>/tmp/gate_$b.err
  if [ -f /tmp/gate_$b.json ]; then
    python3 -c "import json;d=json.load(open('/tmp/gate_$b.json'));print(f'{\"$b\":34} green={d[\"baseline_green\"]} kill={d[\"kill_rate\"]} ({d[\"killed\"]}/{d[\"executed\"]}, surv {d[\"survived\"]})')"
  else echo "$b -> ERR: $(tail -1 /tmp/gate_$b.err)"; fi
done

content.text:
Auto-backgrounded (it's a few minutes of mutation runs). I'll get the kill rates when it completes — that's the gate decision for scaling. The key fix is locked in: **use `.venv/bin/python` directly, never `uv run`** (which tries to sync and hits a stale symlink from the earlier probe). The agents and `mutate.py` already do this; only my manual command slipped.

I'll evaluate the three P0 kill rates the moment they land. If they're at/near the gate, I launch the full 246-target wave for the overnight run. If any are low, I fold them into the patch wave and tune the prompt first. Waiting on that result now.
